text: Testing plays a vital role in software development. Unit tests check small parts of code, like individual functions, to ensure they work correctly. Integration tests go further by verifying that different parts of the system interact properly. Many developers use test-driven development (TDD), where they write tests before the actual code. This approach helps clarify requirements and prevents bugs early. Together, these practices build confidence in the software. When a project has thorough tests, changes can be made more safely, as failures are caught immediately. Ultimately, good testing saves time, reduces frustration, and ensures that the final product is reliable and stable.
